definition: Very, very big. Much bigger than normal!
example: The elephant was enormous compared to the tiny mouse.

definition: Very, very small. So small you might almost miss it!
example: The tiny ladybug landed on my finger.

definition: Not afraid to do something scary or hard.
example: She was brave enough to go down the big slide all by herself.

definition: Wanting to know more about something. Asking lots of questions!
example: The curious kitten peeked inside every box it could find.

definition: Soft and careful. Not rough or loud.
example: Be gentle when you hold the baby rabbit.

definition: Very strong and a little scary. Full of power!
example: The fierce dragon roared loudly from the mountaintop.

definition: Very bright or very smart!
example: The brilliant stars lit up the night sky.

definition: Warm, soft, and comfortable. Like being wrapped in a blanket!
example: We read books in our cozy fort made of pillows.

definition: Easy to break. You have to be very careful with it!
example: The fragile glass ornament could shatter if you dropped it.

definition: Strange and hard to explain. Full of secrets!
example: We found a mysterious door hidden behind the bookshelf.

definition: Very, very old. From a long, long time ago!
example: The ancient castle had been standing for hundreds of years.

definition: So bright and sparkly that it's hard to look away!
example: The dazzling fireworks filled the sky with color.

definition: Moving in a smooth, beautiful way.
example: The graceful ballerina danced across the stage.

definition: Really wonderful and impressive. Almost too amazing to believe!
example: The magnificent rainbow stretched all the way across the sky.

definition: Unusual or strange in an interesting way.
example: The peculiar bird had bright blue feet and a red beak.

definition: Really, really big or really, really great!
example: The crowd let out a tremendous cheer when the team scored.

definition: Very thin or fine, and easy to damage.
example: A delicate butterfly landed on the flower petal.

definition: Extremely angry. Way more than just mad!
example: Mom was furious when the dog chewed up her favorite shoes.

definition: Shining brightly, like something polished and clean.
example: The gleaming treasure chest was full of gold coins.

definition: Not willing to change your mind, no matter what!
example: The stubborn mule would not walk across the bridge.

definition: Happy and full of good feelings!
example: Her cheerful smile made everyone in the room feel better.

definition: In a bad mood and easily annoyed.
example: My little brother is always grumpy when he misses his nap.

definition: So amazing it's hard to believe!
example: The magician did an incredible trick that fooled everyone.

definition: So smooth or wet that it's hard to hold or stand on.
example: The slippery fish kept jumping out of my hands.

definition: Really excited and ready to do something!
example: The eager puppy wagged its tail and ran to the door.

definition: Full of bright, strong colors or energy.
example: The garden was full of vibrant flowers in every color.

definition: Very careful to avoid danger or mistakes.
example: The cautious deer looked both ways before crossing the road.

definition: Tends to trip, drop things, or bump into stuff.
example: The clumsy penguin kept slipping on the ice.

definition: Able to wait calmly without getting upset.
example: She was very patient while waiting in the long line.

definition: Something you cannot see at all!
example: The chameleon was almost invisible against the green leaves.

definition: So tired you can barely move or think!
example: After playing all day, the kids were completely exhausted.

definition: Very special and valuable. Worth a lot!
example: The precious ring had belonged to her grandmother.

definition: So silly it makes you laugh!
example: The clown wore a ridiculous hat covered in bananas.

definition: Extremely hot. So hot it could burn!
example: The scorching sun made the sand too hot to walk on.

definition: Extremely cold. So cold you shiver!
example: We wore thick coats because it was freezing outside.

definition: Very wet and soft, usually in a not-so-great way.
example: My cereal got soggy because I waited too long to eat it.

definition: Glowing with light, like something magical!
example: The luminous moon lit up the path through the forest.

definition: Very wild and scary. Like a powerful animal!
example: The ferocious tiger let out a mighty roar.

definition: Exactly the same. You can't tell them apart!
example: The twins wore identical outfits to school.

definition: A very small version of something bigger.
example: She had a miniature dollhouse that looked just like a real house.

definition: Able to bend easily without breaking.
example: Gymnasts have to be very flexible to do their tricks.

definition: Empty on the inside, like a tube or cave.
example: The owl lived inside a hollow tree trunk.

definition: Twisted and knotted up together in a mess.
example: My hair was so tangled after swimming that I couldn't brush it.

definition: Feeling sleepy and like you might fall asleep.
example: The warm sunshine made the cat feel drowsy.

definition: Shy and a little embarrassed around others.
example: The bashful boy hid behind his mom when visitors came.

definition: Old, torn, and worn out from being used a lot.
example: His ragged teddy bear had been his favorite since he was a baby.

definition: You can see right through it, like glass or water!
example: The transparent jellyfish floated through the ocean.

definition: Soft and puffy, like a cloud or cotton candy!
example: The fluffy kitten curled up on my lap.

definition: Having sharp, rough, uneven edges.
example: Be careful of the jagged rocks along the hiking trail.

definition: Very happy and full of fun!
example: The jolly Santa Claus laughed and waved at everyone.

definition: Grand and beautiful, like a king or queen.
example: The majestic eagle soared high above the mountains.

definition: Playful in a naughty way. Always getting into trouble!
example: The mischievous monkey stole the zookeeper's hat.

definition: Wonderful and impressive. Truly excellent!
example: What a splendid painting you made in art class today!

definition: Spread out all over the place in a messy way.
example: Toys were scattered all over the bedroom floor.

definition: Very mean or evil, like a villain in a story.
example: The wicked witch cast a spell on the sleeping princess.

definition: Easily scared and not very bold.
example: The timid rabbit froze when it heard a noise.

definition: Moving very fast. Quick as the wind!
example: The swift cheetah raced across the grassland.

definition: Dark and sad. Not bright or happy.
example: The gloomy sky was full of dark rain clouds.

definition: Always there for someone. A true friend!
example: My loyal dog waits by the door for me every day after school.

definition: A little bit wet. Not soaked, but not dry either.
example: The grass was still damp from the morning rain.

definition: Shining with warmth and happiness, like the sun!
example: She had a radiant smile on her birthday morning.

definition: Shaky and not steady. About to fall over!
example: The baby took a few wobbly steps before sitting down.

definition: Nicely round and full.
example: We picked plump, juicy strawberries from the garden.

definition: Not afraid to take action or speak up!
example: The bold knight marched right up to the dragon's cave.

definition: Huge and wide, stretching out as far as you can see.
example: The vast ocean seemed to go on forever.

definition: Playful and fun in a magical, unexpected way.
example: The whimsical treehouse had a slide instead of stairs.

definition: Covered in sharp little points that can poke you.
example: The prickly cactus should not be touched with bare hands.

definition: Amazingly huge. Even bigger than enormous!
example: The colossal dinosaur was taller than our house.

definition: Strong and solid. Not easy to break!
example: Dad built a sturdy bookshelf that could hold all our books.

definition: Magical, like something from a fairy tale!
example: The enchanted forest was filled with talking animals.

definition: Any living animal or being.
example: We spotted a tiny creature hiding under the log.

definition: Something very valuable, like gold, jewels, or something you love.
example: The pirates buried their treasure on a secret island.

definition: A mountain that can erupt with hot lava and smoke!
example: The volcano erupted and sent lava flowing down the mountain.

definition: A group of stars that makes a shape or picture in the sky.
example: We looked up and found the Big Dipper constellation.

definition: The remains of a plant or animal from millions of years ago, found in rock.
example: We found a fossil of a leaf in the rocks at the park.

definition: The natural home where an animal or plant lives.
example: The pond is the perfect habitat for frogs and turtles.

definition: An exciting journey or experience full of surprises!
example: Going camping in the woods was a real adventure.

definition: A tool that makes faraway things look closer, especially stars!
example: We used a telescope to look at the moon's craters.

definition: A clear, sparkly rock or mineral with smooth flat sides.
example: The crystal glowed purple in the sunlight.

definition: The bad guy in a story who causes trouble.
example: The villain tried to steal the hero's magic sword.

definition: A journey taken to discover or explore something new.
example: The scientists went on an expedition to the jungle.

definition: Something that gets in your way and you have to get past.
example: The fallen tree was an obstacle on the hiking trail.

definition: A dark shape made when something blocks the light.
example: My shadow got really long in the afternoon sun.

definition: A regular beat or pattern of sounds, like in music.
example: We clapped along to the rhythm of the drums.

definition: A person who designs and plans buildings.
example: The architect drew plans for a brand new school.

definition: A space rock that shoots across the sky like a streak of light!
example: We saw a meteor flash across the night sky.

definition: An animal that hunts other animals for food.
example: The hawk is a predator that catches mice and rabbits.

definition: Colors or patterns that help an animal hide by blending in.
example: The lizard's camouflage made it look like a rock.

definition: A tool with a needle that always points north to help you find your way.
example: The explorer used a compass so she wouldn't get lost.

definition: A really big, powerful snowstorm with lots of wind.
example: The blizzard covered everything in a thick blanket of snow.

definition: A deep valley with steep rocky walls on both sides.
example: We stood at the edge and looked down into the huge canyon.

definition: A special room where scientists do experiments.
example: The scientist mixed colorful liquids in the laboratory.

definition: A tricky question or puzzle that you have to figure out.
example: Can you solve this riddle? What has hands but can't clap?

definition: Someone who protects and watches over another person or thing.
example: The knight was the guardian of the castle gates.

definition: Something you think you see, but it's not really there.
example: In the hot desert, the traveler saw a mirage that looked like water.

definition: A huge amount of snow that suddenly slides down a mountain.
example: The loud noise started an avalanche on the snowy mountain.

definition: Sweet juice inside flowers that bees and butterflies drink.
example: The bee buzzed from flower to flower, sipping nectar.

definition: The path something takes as it moves around something else in space.
example: The Earth travels in an orbit around the sun.

definition: The line far away where the sky seems to meet the ground or ocean.
example: We watched the sun sink below the horizon at the beach.

definition: A piece of land that sticks out into the water, with water on three sides.
example: Florida is a peninsula surrounded by the ocean.

definition: One of the things you mix together to make food or something else.
example: Flour is an important ingredient in making cookies.

definition: A silky covering a caterpillar makes around itself before becoming a butterfly.
example: The caterpillar spun a cocoon and came out as a butterfly!

definition: A confusing maze with lots of twists and turns.
example: We got lost in the labyrinth made of tall corn stalks.

definition: A powerful spinning windstorm shaped like a funnel.
example: The tornado spun across the field, picking up everything in its path.

definition: A strong covering worn to protect the body in battle.
example: The knight put on shiny armor before the jousting match.

definition: A special piece of metal that pulls other metal things toward it.
example: The magnet stuck to the refrigerator and held up my drawing.

definition: A tall tube on top of a building where smoke goes out.
example: Smoke rose from the chimney on the cold winter night.

definition: A long journey, especially by ship or through space.
example: The sailors set off on a voyage across the Atlantic Ocean.

definition: An image you see in a mirror, water, or shiny surface.
example: The deer saw its own reflection in the still lake.

definition: Something brand new that someone creates or builds for the first time.
example: The telephone was an amazing invention that changed the world.

definition: The long feelers on top of an insect's head that help it sense things.
example: The ant waved its antennae to find the trail of food.

definition: One of the seven huge areas of land on Earth.
example: Africa is a continent with many different countries.

definition: A giant group of billions of stars, gas, and dust in space.
example: Our solar system is inside a galaxy called the Milky Way.

definition: A big statue or building made to remember something important.
example: We visited the monument of the Statue of Liberty in New York.

definition: An invisible gas in the air that we need to breathe to stay alive.
example: Trees help make oxygen so people and animals can breathe.

definition: A tool that makes tiny things look big enough to see.
example: Under the microscope, a drop of water was full of tiny creatures.

definition: How hot or cold something is.
example: The temperature outside was so high that the ice cream melted fast.

definition: All the bones inside a body that give it shape.
example: The museum had a skeleton of a real dinosaur!

definition: Everything you can see when you look out over an area of land.
example: The landscape was covered in rolling green hills and flowers.

definition: A large group of people living together with their own rules and culture.
example: The ancient Egyptian civilization built the great pyramids.

definition: A sound that bounces back to you, like when you shout in a cave.
example: We shouted into the canyon and heard our echo come back.

definition: A friend who goes with you and keeps you company.
example: My dog is my favorite companion on long walks.

definition: Chasing after something you really want.
example: The dog ran in pursuit of the squirrel across the yard.

definition: A long piece of music played by a big group of musicians.
example: We went to the concert hall to hear the orchestra play a symphony.

definition: A long time with no rain, when everything gets very dry.
example: During the drought, the grass turned brown and the pond dried up.

definition: To find something new that you didn't know about before.
example: We discovered a secret path behind the old playground.

definition: To go around a new place and see what's there!
example: Let's explore the tide pools and see what creatures we find.

definition: To talk very, very softly so only one person can hear.
example: She had to whisper the secret so nobody else could hear.

definition: To do something special and fun because something good happened!
example: We had a big party to celebrate my birthday.

definition: To make something new that didn't exist before.
example: She used clay to create a beautiful little sculpture.

definition: To run very fast, like a horse!
example: The horse began to gallop across the open field.

definition: To jump suddenly on something to catch it!
example: The cat was ready to pounce on the toy mouse.

definition: To sleep through the whole winter, like some animals do.
example: Bears hibernate in their caves when it gets cold.

definition: To fly high up in the sky without flapping much.
example: The eagle spread its wings and began to soar above the valley.

definition: To disappear completely, like magic!
example: The magician made the coin vanish from his hand.

definition: To shake a little because you're scared, cold, or nervous.
example: The little mouse began to tremble when it saw the cat.

definition: To trip or almost fall while walking or running.
example: I stumbled over a rock but caught myself before falling.

definition: To change completely into something different.
example: The caterpillar will transform into a beautiful butterfly.

definition: To eat something really fast because you're so hungry!
example: The hungry wolf could devour a whole meal in seconds.

definition: To burst out suddenly, like a volcano shooting lava!
example: The volcano could erupt at any moment with hot lava.

definition: To watch something very carefully to learn about it.
example: The scientist liked to observe the birds building their nests.

definition: To build or put something together.
example: We used blocks to construct a tall tower in class.

definition: To make a picture in your mind of something that isn't real yet.
example: Close your eyes and imagine you're flying through the clouds!

definition: To fall down or cave in suddenly.
example: The tower of blocks began to collapse when I bumped the table.

definition: To slide along the ground in a smooth, wavy way, like a snake.
example: We watched the snake slither through the tall grass.

definition: To throw or spread things in many different directions.
example: The wind made the leaves scatter all across the yard.

definition: To break into lots of tiny pieces all at once.
example: The glass ball fell off the table and began to shatter.

definition: To keep someone or something safe from harm.
example: The mother bird will protect her babies from danger.

definition: To move from one place to another when the seasons change.
example: Geese migrate south when winter comes.

definition: To think really hard about one thing and block out everything else.
example: I need to concentrate on my spelling test and not look around.

definition: To show someone how to do something.
example: The teacher will demonstrate how to mix colors to make green.

definition: To rain very lightly in tiny drops.
example: It started to drizzle, so we put on our raincoats.

definition: To light something up and make it bright.
example: Hundreds of candles illuminate the cave and make it glow.

definition: To find your way from one place to another.
example: We used a map to navigate through the forest trails.

definition: To work together with others to get something done.
example: We all had to cooperate to build the biggest sandcastle ever.

definition: To keep yourself steady so you don't fall over.
example: She tried to balance on one foot without wobbling.

definition: To save someone or something from danger.
example: The firefighter rushed in to rescue the kitten from the tree.

definition: To sit on something high up, like a bird on a branch.
example: The parrot liked to perch on top of the bookshelf.

definition: To put things in a certain order or pattern.
example: She liked to arrange her crayons from lightest to darkest.

definition: To draw something quickly with simple lines.
example: I used a pencil to sketch a picture of our dog.

definition: To start growing, like a little plant coming out of a seed.
example: After a week, the bean seeds began to sprout tiny green leaves.

definition: To control which way something goes, like a bike or a boat.
example: She learned to steer her bicycle around the cones.

definition: To walk with short steps while rocking side to side, like a penguin!
example: The ducks waddle down to the pond every morning.

definition: To shake a little because you're cold or scared.
example: Standing in the snow made me shiver from head to toe.

definition: To offer to help without being asked or paid.
example: I decided to volunteer to clean up the park after school.

definition: To dig a hole or tunnel in the ground.
example: The rabbit likes to burrow under the fence to get to the garden.

definition: To make something look bigger than it really is.
example: This magnifying glass can magnify a tiny bug so we can study it.

definition: To create or produce something.
example: Wind turbines generate electricity from the wind.

definition: To beat something or overcome a big challenge!
example: She worked hard to conquer her fear of the dark.

definition: To walk very quietly on the tips of your toes.
example: We had to tiptoe past the sleeping baby's room.

definition: To turn from water into invisible gas and float away.
example: Puddles evaporate when the sun heats them up.

definition: To pause because you're not sure what to do.
example: She started to hesitate at the edge of the diving board.

definition: To move around quietly, looking for something to catch.
example: The cat likes to prowl through the garden at night.

definition: To soak something up, like a sponge soaks up water.
example: The towel can absorb all the water from the puddle.

definition: To speak very softly, almost too quiet to hear.
example: I could hear the students murmur during the test.

definition: To copy what someone else does or says.
example: My little sister likes to mimic everything I do.

definition: To keep trying even when something is really hard.
example: You have to persevere and keep practicing to learn new things.

definition: To move quickly in a messy, hurried way, often using your hands and feet.
example: The kids scramble up the rocky hill to reach the top first.

definition: To grow strong and healthy, or to do really well!
example: The flowers flourish in the warm spring sunshine.

definition: To fly down quickly and suddenly to grab something.
example: The owl will swoop down to catch a mouse in the field.

definition: In a fast way. Not taking much time at all!
example: The rabbit quickly hopped away when it heard a noise.

definition: In a soft, careful way. Not rough at all.
example: She gently placed the baby bird back in its nest.

definition: Without making much noise. Very softly.
example: He quietly closed the door so he wouldn't wake anyone.

definition: In a way that shows you are not afraid.
example: She bravely stood up in front of the whole class to read her poem.

definition: Paying close attention so nothing goes wrong.
example: He carefully carried the glass of water so it wouldn't spill.

definition: With lots of excitement and wanting to do something right away!
example: The puppy eagerly ran to the door when it heard the leash.

definition: In a smooth, beautiful, and elegant way.
example: The swan glided gracefully across the still lake.

definition: In a very strong and powerful way.
example: The wind blew fiercely during the big storm.

definition: In a strange, secretive way that makes you wonder.
example: The old door mysteriously opened all by itself.

definition: In a wild, panicked, hurried way.
example: She searched frantically for her lost homework before school.

definition: In a calm way, without getting upset about waiting.
example: The dog sat patiently while we put on its leash.

definition: Very, very fast. In a hurry!
example: The hummingbird flapped its wings rapidly to hover in the air.

definition: Without making any sound at all.
example: The owl flew silently through the dark night sky.

definition: After a long time. It happens, but not right away.
example: If you keep practicing, you'll eventually learn to ride a bike.

definition: All the time, without ever stopping.
example: The puppy was constantly wagging its tail.

definition: Very carefully, trying to avoid danger.
example: The cat cautiously crept toward the strange noise.

definition: In a very bright or very clever way.
example: The stars shone brilliantly in the clear night sky.

definition: In an awkward way, bumping into things or dropping stuff.
example: He clumsily knocked over the cup while reaching for a cookie.

definition: In a proud, winning way, like you just did something great!
example: She raised her trophy triumphantly above her head.

definition: Doing something even though you really don't want to.
example: He reluctantly put away his toys when it was time for bed.
